playlist_title: Fifty Ways to Buy Instaluvy: Cheap Love Always Does the Trick

voices.fraktur: besame mucho cluster — medieval, continental, candlelight
voices.script: la vie en rose cluster — cursive, napkin, three in the morning
voices.italic: unruly laundresses originals — already in motion
voices.monospace: one-offs and strange arrivals — home register of the console

iHfFbXhBYdI.title: SECOND HAND — The Unruly Laundresses
iHfFbXhBYdI.text:
You are now inside the thing that made this playlist.
The Unruly Laundresses are from Lewisburg, Kentucky,
which is not on most maps that matter to the people
who make maps that matter.
This is intentional.
Something was owned. Something was released.
Something waits on a hanger for the right person to complete it.
You are the right person.
Or you are not.
Either way the hanger is there.

toSBTUyqzyw.title: Besame Mucho — Wilfried Vermeulen (ska/reggae, Netherlands)
toSBTUyqzyw.text:
Wilfried Vermeulen has 9 monthly listeners on Spotify.
You are probably one of them now.
The 1970s wallpaper in this video
has been waiting thirty years
for someone to notice it properly.
Notice it properly.
Consuelo Velazquez wrote Besame Mucho in 1940
when she was nineteen and had never been kissed.
This is the first thing you should know about it.
The second thing is that it became one of the most recorded songs in history.
The song has been kissed by ten thousand mouths
and remains, impossibly,
about longing for a kiss that hasn't happened yet.

2USNek_tWjI.title: La vie en rose — Iggy Pop
2USNek_tWjI.text:
Iggy Pop does this in 2 minutes and 17 seconds.
Which is basically a sprinting distance for Piaf.
It is not delicate.
It is exactly as delicate as it needs to be.
which is not very.
and is also somehow deeply correct.
Iggy Pop at this point in his life
has earned the right to see things through rose.
He also inspired O WORM.
which is also on this playlist.
We will get there.

Tar73Zq7MyE.title: Sukiyaki — Maier Goldberg and Ronen Meir (Hebrew, 1991)
Tar73Zq7MyE.text:
Ue o Muite Aruko.
I look up as I walk, so the tears won't fall.
Kyu Sakamoto recorded this in 1961.
A British jazz promoter renamed it Sukiyaki
because Western audiences would find the real title unpronounceable.
The song was renamed after a beef dish.
It won the Billboard Hot 100 in 1963.
Kyu Sakamoto died in the Japan Airlines disaster of 1985.
The deadliest single-aircraft accident in history.
The song, now orphaned, kept traveling.
This is the Hebrew version. 1991.
Find the lady on the left.
Listen again.
The song is now in its third language, third country, third meaning.
It does not protest this.
It has learned to hold everything.

QO9eZlcUpWU.title: Gute Grunde, um zu gehen — Dahlia Lavi
QO9eZlcUpWU.text:
Dahlia Lavi was an Israeli singer, actress,
and in 1966 a Bond girl.
The Silencers.
Which is already more than most people
have on their resume.
Gute Grunde um zu Gehen means good reasons to leave.
She delivers it with the serene authority
of someone who has already decided
and is simply being kind enough to explain.
blackSocks confesses to not yet having listened
carefully to the lyrics
and notes that even if his reading is entirely wrong,
Dahlia Lavi would give precisely zero schtiks about it,
because blackSocks isn't even close to being in her league.
The ibis finds this an admirably honest assessment.

Ay9Jfru-n0I.title: CHEAP LOVE — The Unruly Laundresses
Ay9Jfru-n0I.text:
This is the thesis statement.
Cheap love always does the trick.
This is not a consolation prize.
It is not a compromise.
It is a phenomenological claim
about the structure of love
and the economy of grace.
The playlist has been building toward this
and will spend the rest of its runtime
explaining why it is true.
It is true.

92W3tJyQY0E.title: Besame Mucho — Inspector (lyrics video)
92W3tJyQY0E.text:
Inspector does it straight.
Mexican rock.
Gorgeous and earnest.
A lyrics video that trusts the words to do the work.
The words do the work.
Three versions of Besame Mucho on this playlist.
Each one means something different.
None of them are wrong.
This is also true of people.

c8BPk254LM8.title: Danny Chan — pink life (Cantonese)
c8BPk254LM8.text:
This video would be utterly horrid
if it weren't made so long ago,
and if the gentleman were still alive,
and if in the 2020s it weren't so utterly funny.
The exaggerated 80s-style dance moves —
sort of like a ballerina attempting her first few steps at disco —
from a smiling Cantonese man singing about love,
but looking like his body is trying to compete with Michael Jackson,
only with a distinct whiff of the Lawrence Welk Show.
Chan died in 1993 at thirty-five.
The official cause was an accidental fall.
Hong Kong has never quite stopped mourning him.
The ibis finds the song the exact color of hope
that still contains the memory of something lost.
blackSocks disagrees.
Both readings are correct.
Ai ya.

Pjf_hemwVD8.title: Sukiyaki
Pjf_hemwVD8.text:
The song keeps traveling.
This version does not explain itself.
Listen to what the translation kept
and what it let go.
Every translation is a theory of what matters.
This one has a theory.
You can hear it if you listen
for what is not there.

JfH_fKvonr0.title: Fifty Ways to Leave Your Lover — Pavel Bobek (Czech)
JfH_fKvonr0.text:
The Pavel Bobek version of Fifty Ways to Leave Your Lover
is a thing that should not work
and absolutely does.
Find the lady on the left in this video
and you will be able to hear her better.
Notice the funky way mirroring is used
to make you feel like you are watching it live.
Also: check out all the silver-haired Lotharios.
There are moments in this video
where Bobek is more Paul Simon than Paul Simon.
This is uncanny in the way that only Czech interpretations
of American pop songs from the 1970s can be uncanny.
Which is to say: completely, and without apology.

DhTlyCmCof4.title: O WORM
DhTlyCmCof4.text:
53 seconds.
Iggy Pop inspired this.
The ibis has nothing to add
and stands by this position.

_LUyZ513Rsk.title: Kysstu mig — Kiss Me (Icelandic)
_LUyZ513Rsk.text:
Icelandic.
It means kiss me.
You knew that before you read this.
Some things cross every language
without needing a translator.
This is one of them.
The playlist knows.

vxlDUY_wsgw.title: La Vie En Rose — La Voz de Mi Trompeta Mariachi Sol de Mexico de Jose Hernandez
vxlDUY_wsgw.text:
So drippy it's trippy.
It arrives slowly, like something poured.
Prayer Group X, whose name derives partly
from the Greek pharmakeia —
the same root as pharmacy, as poison, as enchantment,
as the Thoth and Thamus story
about writing as a drug that makes you think you remember
when actually you have only read —
considers itself in some sense
a dealer of exactly this kind of thing.
Drippy and trippy.
We are not sorry.

XJHU6x5NTmU.title: Sukiyaki — live at Absolute Blue Ikebukuro
XJHU6x5NTmU.text:
Live. Ikebukuro. The song in its home country,
performed for people who know what it is,
who know what it was,
who remember.
The beef dish name never made it back here.
Here it is still Ue o Muite Aruko.
I look up as I walk so the tears won't fall.
Four versions of this song on the playlist.
This one is the one that stayed home
while the others traveled.
Listen to the room.

_tCp4dzzzaM.title: Fifty Ways to Leave Your Lover — Paul Simon
_tCp4dzzzaM.text:
One of the most extraordinary drum intros in pop music history.
The song is nominally about leaving
but actually about being left.
Which Simon perhaps knew and perhaps didn't.
The playlist is titled after this song.
This was always going to be complicated.
Slip out the back, Jack.
Make a new plan, Stan.
Don't need to be coy, Roy.
Drop off the key, Lee.
Just listen, Stan.
Not yet.
Not yet.

HNooRxGGnbI.title: The Eye Hand SLAM SLAM — The Unruly Laundresses
HNooRxGGnbI.text:
This is what happens
when the thesis meets the world at high speed.
The ibis cannot explain this one
and finds that appropriate.
Some things in a playlist
exist to remind you
that the playlist is not entirely in control.
This is one of those things.
SLAM SLAM.

DnMAyTOqgPo.title: Besame Mucho — Los Skeleteros (mariachi punk)
DnMAyTOqgPo.text:
Mariachi punk.
Which is either a genre or a theology.
Prayer Group X takes no position on this
except to say: yes.
The third and final Besame Mucho on this playlist.
Three versions of the same kiss.
Each one means something different.
None of them are wrong.
Los Skeleteros have understood something
about the relationship between the sacred
and the loud
that most seminaries have not yet addressed.

4dIy_vW4yqc.title: La Vie En Rose — The Macarons Project (live at Monarch Studios)
4dIy_vW4yqc.text:
Stripped back.
Just voice and guitar.
The version that reminds you
the song is about a specific Tuesday afternoon
when someone looked at you
and you saw everything differently.
Not a Tuesday in general.
A specific Tuesday.
You know which one.
The Macarons Project knows too.

_4mWyYLXmXM.title: Sukiyaki — Limousine
_4mWyYLXmXM.text:
blackSocks finds what Limousine did here brilliant.
So brilliant you wouldn't even know it was Sukiyaki
unless they told you with the track name.
The melody has been dissolved
and reconstructed into something
that feels like memory rather than song.
It is the ghost of the original.
Which is perhaps the most honest version of all.
Four ways. One ghost.
The ghost is this one.

UWy2ke2ergA.title: Fifty Ways to Leave Your Lover — FMT feat. Camilla
UWy2ke2ergA.text:
FMT — probably Frank Meyer-Thurn,
no shame in thinking otherwise,
Google AI also gets this wrong.
They have 39 monthly listeners on Spotify.
Their cover of Leonard Cohen's Suzanne is also very good.
Go figger.
The reason this playlist exists,
the reason PanderThings exist,
the reason blackSocks distributes HTML files
person-to-person by AirDrop
instead of posting things to social media,
is precisely because the attention economy
has decided that 39 is a negligible number
and 9 is a rounding error.
The ibis disagrees.
So does cheap love.

q0gwn7ah7kA.title: Throw Away the Receipt — The Unruly Laundresses
q0gwn7ah7kA.text:
The economy of love rendered as policy.
Throw away the receipt.
There are no returns on this.
There is no exchange.
There is no store credit.
This is not a flaw in the system.
This is the system working correctly.
Cheap love always does the trick.
Throw away the receipt.

tk7jXVnO4co.title: Ethernet Cuckoo Amen — The Unruly Laundresses
tk7jXVnO4co.text:
77 seconds.
The ibis cannot explain this one.
This is different from not being able to explain O WORM.
O WORM is inexplicable in a way that feels complete.
Ethernet Cuckoo Amen is inexplicable in a way that feels
like something is still being worked out.
Amen.
The ibis waits.

18iKauFkzlA.title: BUY INSTALUVY — The Unruly Laundresses
18iKauFkzlA.text:
28 seconds.
The shortest thing on the playlist.
The entire argument compressed into a jingle.
The playlist has just spent approximately two hours
explaining why cheap love always does the trick.
This is the conclusion.
It does not elaborate.
It does not need to.
Cheap love always does the trick.
The ibis looks at you.
The playlist is over.
Go buy something local.
